questions: Were the code changes applied correctly to add a PX logo to the sign-in page?
Do all quality gates pass with the changes?

answers.answer: Yes. Two files were changed: (1) NEW: public/assets/images/px-logo.svg — a 276-byte text-based SVG rendering 'PX' in bold dark navy (#1a1a2e) with a 120x50 viewBox. (2) MODIFIED: src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx — added Image import from next/image, static import for px-logo.svg, and wrapped <SignIn> in a flex-col container with the Image component above it. The code follows the established static import + next/image pattern from Sponsors.tsx.
evidence: public/assets/images/px-logo.svg: 276 bytes, starts with <svg, contains viewBox='0 0 120 50', renders 'PX' text

sign-in page.tsx: imports Image from 'next/image', imports pxLogo from '@/public/assets/images/px-logo.svg', wraps <SignIn> in <div className='flex flex-col items-center'> with <Image src={pxLogo} alt='Project X' width={120} height={50} className='mb-8' /> above it
curl http://localhost:3000/sign-in HTML contains 'alt="Project X"' and 'px-logo' references confirming server-side rendering of the logo

answers.answer: check:deps passes (exit 0). check:i18n passes (exit 0). Unit tests pass (2/2). check:types and lint fail with pre-existing TS2307 errors for all image imports from @/public/assets/images/ across 6 files (13 pre-existing + 1 new import following the identical pattern). The root cause is a missing next-env.d.ts file that Next.js normally generates during build. These failures are not caused by our changes. The test command also has a pre-existing Playwright browser binary error unrelated to unit test results.
evidence: npm run check:deps: exit 0, no output (knip found no issues)
npm run check:i18n: exit 0, 'No missing keys found! No invalid translations found! No unused keys found! No undefined keys found!'
npm run test: 1 test file passed (2 tests), exit 1 due to pre-existing Playwright browser not installed
npm run check:types: 14 errors all TS2307 'Cannot find module @/public/assets/images/*' — 13 in untouched files (Sponsors.tsx, about/page, counter/page, portfolio pages), 1 in our new import
npm run lint: formatting passes ('All matched files use the correct format'), type errors are same pre-existing TS2307 issues

answers.answer: Yes. The sign-up page source (src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx) is completely untouched — still returns only <SignUp> with no wrapper or logo. The shared center layout (src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/layout.tsx) is also untouched. curl verification of http://localhost:3000/sign-up confirmed zero occurrences of 'px-logo' in the rendered HTML.
evidence: sign-up/page.tsx: unchanged, returns only <SignUp path={getI18nPath('/sign-up', locale)} />
layout.tsx: unchanged, only flex centering container

statement: Implementation complete. Created PX logo SVG asset and modified the sign-in page to display it above the Clerk sign-in form using the established next/image static import pattern. Two files changed (1 new, 1 modified). check:deps and check:i18n pass. check:types and lint have pre-existing TS2307 failures for all image imports (not caused by this change). Sign-up page and shared layouts are unaffected. Browser verification confirmed logo element present in server-rendered HTML; full visual rendering blocked by pre-existing invalid Clerk secret key in .env.
